findings.where: Opening paragraph before “The problem with tangent lines”
findings.issue: The draft characterizes the tangent line as “the unique line that just touches the curve at a single point,” which is an over-generalized and potentially false description for general curves.
findings.evidence: A tangent line need not intersect the curve only at the point of tangency, and it may cross the curve at the tangency point. The seed’s mathematical backbone defines the tangent by the limiting secant slope, not by single-point contact.
findings.suggestion: Replace the single-point-touch characterization with a limiting-slope/direction description, or qualify it as informal intuition rather than the defining property.

summary: One blocking math finding: the informal opening definition of tangent line overstates the “single point of contact” idea. The rest of the draft is faithful to the seed, includes the direction-brief examples/application/bookend, and avoids the listed forbidden topics.
